message:
fix(cleanup): fail-closed in cleanup_path_has_symlink_below_root unreachable-root arms [coding_agent_session_search-xwzkm]

Three arms previously returned false ("no symlink, safe") when the
walk could not reach the configured root: (a) symlink_metadata error,
(b) Path::parent() returns None, (c) parent == current. The outer
cleanup_target_path_is_safe predicate treats false as "continue,
maybe safe"; a future refactor that removed or weakened the
defense-in-depth canonicalize() check further down would expose the
helper's fail-OPEN behavior as a real symlink-escape vector.

Flip all three arms to return true ("has symlink, unsafe") so the
helper stands on its own as fail-closed. Downstream canonicalize()
continues to provide belt-and-suspenders, but the helper's contract
no longer depends on it.

Three new regression tests call the helper directly:
- metadata error on ancestor (nonexistent path) must report unsafe
- walk exhausts parents without reaching root must report unsafe
- clean walk that reaches root still reports safe (happy-path pin)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
